scope: Exact installed-Blender-5.1 evaluated geometry, face-material assignments, and the observable authored Eevee surface. The filament appearance is a viewport shader, not a sliced toolpath or G-code comparison.

geometry.versionVariance: The archived Blender 4.3 default contains one fewer auto-smooth split vertex while preserving all 8,064 faces and four-decimal local bounds. Installed Blender 5.1.2 and GN-VM agree at 11,163 vertices.

filament.surface: shared Generated-coordinate procedural filament and cross-section reconstruction

mahogany.missingAttributeBehavior: The evaluated structure geometry has no col1 or col2 attributes. Blender evaluates missing Shader Attribute values as black even though the node's UI preview swatch is gray; WebGL now follows the evaluated black fallback.
mahogany.wave: distorted X-band Wave Texture modulated by the Generated-coordinate Noise Texture
mahogany.roughness: authored color ramp evaluated from the mixed Wave/Noise field

printVisualization.exportContract: The evaluated design mesh is the slicer input. Printer-specific layers and G-code require a printer, nozzle, filament, support, infill, and speed profile and cannot be recovered from this viewport material.

capture.blenderEnvironment: bundled studio.exr at strength 0.8, Medium High Contrast, no area lights
capture.browserEnvironment: bundled studio.exr at scene intensity 1 and material envMapIntensity 0.8, rotation pi
capture.environmentInterpretation: The effective environment intensity is 0.8 in both renderers. Three.js separates scene and material environment multipliers, while Blender stores one world strength.

interpretation: Modern Pipe now agrees with Blender on complete evaluated topology, bounds, material-face ownership, the blue filament shell, and the missing-attribute-driven black mahogany structure. Its aligned exposure and occupancy are close, while Eevee and Three.js/PMREM retain different high-frequency noise filtering, bump derivatives, environment sampling, and Principled BRDF response.
